description: Single-session reliability scenarios for PolyPilot. Tests cover every way to interact with a single session: sending messages, tool use, abort, rapid input, session lifecycle, restart recovery, state hygiene, and edge cases. Execute against a running PolyPilot instance using MauiDevFlow CDP commands.

prerequisites.notes: All test sessions MUST point to the PolyPilot repository (PureWeen-PolyPilot). Never use other repos. Never commit, push, or create GitHub issues/PRs during testing. The DevFlow agent port is 9223 (bypass broker with --agent-port 9223). CDP click events require PointerEvent dispatch for Blazor compatibility.

cdpHelpers.clickBlazorButton: btn.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerdown', {bubbles: true, clientX: x, clientY: y})); btn.dispatchEvent(new PointerEvent('pointerup', {bubbles: true, clientX: x, clientY: y})); btn.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', {bubbles: true, clientX: x, clientY: y}));
cdpHelpers.setInputValue: const el = document.getElementById(inputId); el.value = text; el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', {bubbles: true}));
cdpHelpers.sendMessage: 1) Set textarea value via getElementValue-compatible path, 2) PointerEvent click on send-btn[title='Send message']

scenarios.name: Simple message sends and receives a response
scenarios.description: Send 'What is 2+2? Reply with just the number.' and verify the response arrives, IsProcessing clears, and event log shows clean [SEND]→[COMPLETE].
invariants: Response text appears in chat history
No [ERROR] or [WATCHDOG] entries for this session

steps.text: What is 2+2? Reply with just the number.

scenarios.name: Long multi-paragraph prompt processes correctly
scenarios.description: Send a multi-paragraph prompt to verify large inputs don't break processing.
invariants: No truncation or corruption of input

steps.text:
Please analyze the following requirements and provide your thoughts:

1. The system must handle concurrent requests
2. It must maintain session state across restarts
3. Error recovery should be automatic
4. All state transitions must be logged

What design patterns would you recommend?

scenarios.name: Response renders markdown as formatted HTML
scenarios.description: Send a prompt requesting markdown output and verify it renders as HTML.
invariants: Response contains HTML elements (not raw markdown)
Code blocks, headers, or lists render correctly

steps.text: Write a short bullet list of 3 items about testing.

scenarios.name: Code block renders with syntax highlighting
scenarios.description: Request code output and verify it renders in a code block.

steps.text: Write a one-line Python hello world program. Just the code, no explanation.

scenarios.name: Empty send is prevented
scenarios.description: Attempt to send an empty message and verify it's blocked.
invariants: No [SEND] entry in event log for empty message

scenarios.name: Tool execution: file listing completes
scenarios.description: Send a file listing request that requires tool use. Verify tools execute and response contains expected files.
invariants: Tool execution events appear in event log

steps.text: List just the file names in the root directory of this repository. Be brief.

scenarios.name: Tool execution: file read completes
scenarios.description: Request reading a specific file to verify file read tools work.
invariants: Response contains content from the file

steps.text: Read the first line of README.md in this repo and tell me what it says. Be brief.

scenarios.name: Multi-round tool use completes without getting stuck
scenarios.description: Send a request requiring multiple tool calls in sequence. Verify all rounds complete.
invariants: Multiple TurnStart/TurnEnd cycles in event log

steps.text: Find the PolyPilot.slnx file and tell me how many project references it contains. Be brief.

scenarios.name: Processing indicator shows during tool use
scenarios.description: During tool execution, verify the UI shows working/tool call indicators.

steps.text: Search the codebase for all files containing 'IsProcessing' and count them. Be brief.

steps.note: Allow time for tool execution to start

scenarios.name: Long tool execution doesn't trigger false watchdog
scenarios.description: A prompt requiring extended tool use completes without watchdog interference.
invariants: No [WATCHDOG] kill entries in event log

steps.text: Count the total number of lines across all .cs files in the PolyPilot/Services/ directory. Be brief, just give me the count.

scenarios.name: Abort during 'Thinking...' phase cleans up state
scenarios.description: Send a prompt, abort during the thinking phase. Verify IsProcessing clears and all companion fields reset.
invariants: IsProcessing = false within 5 seconds of abort
Session accepts new input after abort

steps.text: Write a very detailed 5000-word essay about software testing methodologies.

scenarios.name: Abort during tool execution cleans up state
scenarios.description: Send a tool-using prompt, abort during tool execution. Verify clean state.

steps.text: Read every single .cs file in the repository and summarize each one in detail.

steps.note: Wait for tool execution to start

scenarios.name: Session accepts new message after abort
scenarios.description: Abort a session, then immediately send a new prompt. Verify no deadlock.
invariants: New message processes successfully after abort
IsProcessing = false after new message completes

steps.text: Write a very long detailed analysis of every design pattern ever created.
Say 'hello'. Just that one word.

scenarios.name: Abort on idle session doesn't crash
scenarios.description: Try to abort a session that's already idle. Verify no crash or error.

steps.text: Say 'still working'. Just those two words.

scenarios.name: Back-to-back messages both complete
scenarios.description: Send message 1, wait for completion, immediately send message 2. Both should complete.
invariants: Both messages get [COMPLETE] in event log

steps.text: Say 'first'. Just that word.
Say 'second'. Just that word.

scenarios.name: Send during processing is handled correctly
scenarios.description: Send a second message while the first is still processing. Verify no crash, second message eventually processes.
invariants: Both messages eventually complete or second is queued

steps.text: List every .cs file in the PolyPilot/Services directory.
Say 'queued message received'.

scenarios.name: Three rapid messages don't cause permanent stuck state
scenarios.description: Send 3 messages in quick succession. Verify session eventually reaches idle.
invariants: Event log has at least one [COMPLETE]

steps.text: Say 'one'.
Say 'two'.
Say 'three'.

scenarios.name: Create new session appears in sidebar
scenarios.description: Create a new session and verify it appears in the session list.

scenarios.name: Session with repo context has tool access
scenarios.description: Create a session pointed at PolyPilot repo, verify it can use tools.

steps.text: What repository am I in? Be brief.

scenarios.name: Switching sessions preserves each one's history
scenarios.description: Switch between two sessions and verify each retains its own chat history.
invariants: Each session shows its own history after switching

scenarios.name: Closed session doesn't appear after removal
scenarios.description: Close a session and verify it's removed from the sidebar.

scenarios.name: Chat history persists across interactions
scenarios.description: Send a message, then verify the chat database has the message stored.
invariants: History survives session switch and return

steps.text: Say 'persistence test 12345'. Just those exact words.

scenarios.name: Session restores correctly after app relaunch
scenarios.description: Send a message, relaunch the app, verify session is restored with history.
invariants: Session appears in sidebar after relaunch
Session is idle (not stuck in processing)

scenarios.name: Idle session doesn't get '(resumed)' tag after relaunch
scenarios.description: After relaunch, idle sessions should not have phantom '(resumed)' markers.
invariants: All sessions have their original names

scenarios.name: No duplicate or phantom sessions after relaunch
scenarios.description: After relaunch, verify no duplicate sessions appeared.
invariants: Session count matches pre-relaunch count (±1 for any cleaned up)

scenarios.name: Session is functional after relaunch
scenarios.description: After relaunch, send a message to a restored session and verify it completes.

steps.text: Say 'post relaunch OK'. Just those exact words.

scenarios.name: No sessions stuck in IsProcessing after relaunch
scenarios.description: After relaunch, verify no sessions are stuck in processing state.
invariants: Zero visible stop buttons (no sessions processing)

steps.note: Wait for watchdog to clear any stuck sessions

scenarios.name: IsProcessing is false on all idle sessions
scenarios.description: Check that every session in the list has IsProcessing = false when no messages are being sent.

scenarios.name: Every [SEND] has a matching [COMPLETE] or [ABORT]
scenarios.description: Audit the event diagnostics log. Every [SEND] entry should have a corresponding completion.
invariants: Number of [SEND] entries ≤ [COMPLETE] + [ABORT] entries (for test sessions)

scenarios.name: Watchdog doesn't fire for normally-completing sessions
scenarios.description: Verify that sessions completing normally don't trigger watchdog entries.

scenarios.name: Processing indicator shows during work and clears after
scenarios.description: Send a message, verify processing indicator appears, then clears after completion.

steps.text: List the top-level directories in this repository.

steps.note: May or may not catch the processing window

scenarios.name: No phantom 'Thinking...' after session completes
scenarios.description: After a message completes, verify the session is truly idle with no lingering indicators.
invariants: No thinking/working text visible for session
Input is enabled and accepts typing
Send button is not a stop button

scenarios.name: Special characters in prompt don't cause issues
scenarios.description: Send a prompt with quotes, backticks, unicode, and special characters.

steps.text: Echo this back: Hello 'world' "test" `code` <tag> & © ñ 🎉

scenarios.name: Session works after model switch
scenarios.description: Switch the model for a session, then send a message. Verify it completes.
invariants: Message sends and completes with new model

steps.note: Switch to a different available model via UI dropdown

steps.text: Say 'model switch OK'. Just those words.

scenarios.name: Old sessions load without errors
scenarios.description: Verify that existing sessions from before testing load and don't cause errors.
invariants: No error messages or crash indicators
